zh.Ingestre, Staffordshire, England: 英國英格蘭斯塔福爾英格斯特
英國英格蘭斯塔福爾英格斯特
英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當William De Cheywynd II於大約1355年出生在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾英格斯特時，他的父親William De Chetwynd I30歲，母親Matilda De Verdon27歲。他於1364年與Aline De St. Paul結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1395年2月享年41歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William De Cheywynd II于大约1355年出生在英国英格兰斯塔福爾英格斯特时，他的父亲William De Chetwynd I30岁，母亲Matilda De Verdon27岁。他于1364年与Aline De St. Paul结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1395年2月享年41岁。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Cheywynd
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Chatwin
meanings: English (Warwickshire and Staffordshire): habitational name from a place in Shropshire called Chetwynd, from the Old English personal name Ceatta + Old English (ge)wind ‘winding ascent’.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Japanese: written 井伊 ‘well’ and ‘that’, the name one of Japan's great daimyō families, originally based in Tōtōmi (now part of Shizuoka prefecture), later in Kōzuke (now Gunma prefecture) and Ōmi (now Shiga prefecture). Other variants of the name substitute either character with similar sounding characters such as 飯 ‘cooked rice’, 居 ‘residence’, and so forth. It was formerly Romanized as Iyi. — Note: In the population figure published by the US Census Bureau, the Roman number II (meaning ‘the second’, i.e. ‘the younger’ of the two bearers of the name) is apparently also counted as a surname Ii.

History: Ii Naomasa (1561–1602) was awarded lands in Kōzuke and Ōmi for his service in the civil wars that won the shōgunate (military dictatorship) for Tokugawa Ieyasu. His family built the beautiful castle of Hikone, which still stands. His descendant Ii Naosuke (1815–1860) ruled Japan for two years as Tairō (Great Elder) and played a major part in signing treaties that opened Japan to foreign trade at the end of the Tokugawa Shōgunate. He was assassinated by adherents of the anti-foreign party.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William De Cheywynd II was born about 1355, in Ingestre, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, William De Chetwynd I, was 30 and his mother, Matilda De Verdon, was 27. He married Aline De St. Paul in 1364. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 3 daughters. He died in February 1395, at the age of 41.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William De Cheywynd II est né environ 1355, à Ingestre, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William De Chetwynd I, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Matilda De Verdon, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Aline De St. Paul en 1364. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé en février 1395, à l’âge de 41 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als William De Cheywynd II um 1355 in Ingestre, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater William De Chetwynd I 30 und seine Mutter Matilda De Verdon 27. Er hat 1364 Aline De St. Paul geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb im Februar 1395 im Alter von 41 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William De Cheywynd II è nato il circa 1355 nel Ingestre, Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, William De Chetwynd I, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Matilda De Verdon, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Aline De St. Paul nel 1364. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 3 figlie. Morì nel febbraio 1395, all'età di 41 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: William De Cheywynd IIが約1355年にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアインジェストアで生まれた時，父William De Chetwynd Iは30歳，母Matilda De Verdonは27歳であった。1364年にAline De St. Paulと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1395年2月に41歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William De Cheywynd II(이)가 대략 1355년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 잉스트르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William De Chetwynd I(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Matilda De Verdon(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1364년 Aline De St. Paul(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1395년 2월 41세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William De Cheywynd II родился около 1355 г. в местности ингестр, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени William De Chetwynd I было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Matilda De Verdon, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Aline De St. Paul в 1364 г. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он умер февраль 1395 г. в возрасте 41 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William De Cheywynd II nació aproximadamente 1355, en Ingestre, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, William De Chetwynd I, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Matilda De Verdon, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Aline De St. Paul en 1364. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió en febrero de 1395, a la edad de 41 años.
